headline: OAuth 2.0
description: OAuth 2.0 is an authorization framework standardized as IETF RFC 6749 (2012) that enables third-party applications to obtain limited access to a user's resources without exposing their credentials. It powers virtually all "Sign in with Google/GitHub/Apple" flows and is the foundation of modern API security.

citation.name: OAuth 2.1 and OpenID Connect: The Modern Identity Stack (2025)
